steps.text: Submit your comments, identified by Docket ID No. EPA–HQ–OAR–2022–0491, at https://www.regulations.gov or the other methods identified in the ADDRESSES section.
Use a heated head pump that is constructed from materials non-reactive with the analytes of interest.
Connect cylinder regulators to the gas manifold.
Follow the detector linearity verification procedures in proposed revised section 11.2.
Follow the data storage requirements in new proposed section 11.8.
Follow the QA spike requirements in new proposed section 11.4.
Follow the sampling procedures specified in new proposed section 11.5.
Record a post-run CTS.
Assign unique file names.
Store two copies of interferograms and spectra.
Prepare sample spectrum documentation.
Verify the gas cell pathlength according to the procedures in revised section 10.6.4.
Perform the QA spike procedure using a certified standard or a non-certified standard.
Perform sampling procedures, including performing a stratification check.
Compare the pre- and post-test CTS spectra.
Record and report according to the revised requirements.
Store digital data incorporating the requirements from section 8.4.
Flow the CTS gas through the cell and verify that the measured concentration is stable to within the uncertainty of the gas standard.
Record the spectrum.
Measure the linewidth of appropriate CTS band(s) to verify instrument resolution, or alternatively, compare CTS spectra to a reference CTS spectrum, if available, measured at the nominal resolution.
Conduct a QA spike per the instructions in section 11.4 of this method.
Sample per section 11.5 of this method.
Monitor the signal transmittance while sampling.
Obtain a new background spectrum.
Record another CTS spectrum.
Perform post-run QA per section 9.1.2 of this method.
Observe the single beam instrument response in the frequency region below the detector cutoff (usually <400 cm¥1), where the detector response is known to be zero.
Verify that the detector response is 'flat' and equal to zero in this region, or at least 100 times less than the peak signal in the entire spectrum.
If the response is not linear, decrease the aperture or attenuate the IR beam, and repeat the linearity check until the detector response is linear.
Verify the gas cell pathlength of your instrument by following the procedure found in section 10.6.4 of this method.
Choose one of two options and perform the standard addition procedure listed in section 9.3 of this method.

preconditions.text: The pump is positioned upstream of the distribution manifold and FTIR system.
The method has been validated for each of the target analytes at the source.
If the transmittance (relative to background) changes by 5% or more in any analytical spectral region

constraints.text: Provide safety data sheets for gas standards to all personnel using the method.
Regulator should be constructed of the appropriate materials that minimize analyte adsorption and reactivity.

The calibration gas manifold must provide accurate dilution of the calibration gas as necessary, monitor calibration gas pressure, and introduce analyte spikes into the sample stream (prior to the particulate filter) at a precise and known flowrate.
A filter (required) must be connected at the outlet of the heated probe and rated for 99% removal efficiency of 1 micron (μm) aerodynamic particulate.
The sample line must be heated to prevent sample condensation, and made of stainless steel, PTFE, or other material that minimizes adsorption of analytes.
The sample line length must be the minimum necessary to reach sampling locations.
The sample pump must be leak-free with a bypass valve, capable of producing a sample flow rate equal to 5 cell volumes per sample cycle.
The sampling probe must be capable of sustained heating to prevent water condensation and adsorption of analytes.
Tubing must be PTFE, 316-stainless steel, or other inert material, of suitable length and diameter.
